authors: Devulder J, De Laat M, Van Bastelaere M, Rolly G
title: Spinal Cord Stimulation: A Valuable Treatment for Chronic Failed Back Surgery Patients
journal_name: Journal of Pain and Symptom Management
study_question: Evaluate long-term results and cost-effectiveness of spinal cord stimulation for chronic failed back surgery syndrome.
population_assessed: 69 patients with chronic failed back surgery syndrome (CFBSS) treated with SCS.
outcome_measures: Return to work, drug intake, pain evaluation, medical costs.
inclusion_criteria: CFBSS, no other surgical option, no psychological impairment, successful 2-week trial stimulation.
answer_to_study_question: SCS is a valuable, potentially cost-effective treatment for CFBSS, with 43/69 patients achieving good pain relief.
